content.problemStatement:
I have no compassion, I only exist to collect the souls of the living.

On Halloween, Grim Reaper wants to reap souls from houses.

There are NNN houses. The iii-th of them has a safety level of AiA_iAi​ and has BiB_iBi​ people living in it.

The Reaper has an ability level, denoted LLL. Initially, L=0L = 0L=0.

When the Reaper visits a house, the following two things happen in order:


If L≥AiL \geq A_iL≥Ai​, the Reaper will reap the souls of all BiB_iBi​ people living in the house.

If L<AiL \lt A_iL<Ai​, this does not happen.
Next, LLL becomes equal to AiA_iAi​, the safety level of the house.

This change occurs whether souls were reaped or not.
The Reaper will visit each house exactly once, in some order.

However, there are MMM special houses, which must be visited in a specific order.

You are given this information as an array C=[C1,C2,…,CM]C = [C_1, C_2, \ldots, C_M]C=[C1​,C2​,…,CM​] containing MMM distinct integers — where the CiC_iCi​-th house must be visited before the Ci+1C_{i+1}Ci+1​-th house.

Find the **maximum** possible number of souls the Reaper can reap, if the order of visiting houses is chosen optimally under the given conditions.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of four lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of houses and the size of array CCC respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the safeties of the houses.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ — the number of people in each house.
The fourth line of each test case contains MMM space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CMC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_MC1​,C2​,…,CM​, representing the array CCC.



In particular, if M=0M = 0M=0, the fourth line of input will be blank.



Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of souls that reaper can collect.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Reaper has no choice but to visit houses in order [3,4,1,2,5][3, 4, 1, 2, 5][3,4,1,2,5]. The process is as follows:

Initially, L=0L = 0L=0.
For house 333, A3=2>0A_3 = 2 \gt 0A3​=2>0, so no souls are reaped. After this, LLL changes to 222.
For house 444, A4=1≤2A_4 = 1 \leq 2A4​=1≤2, so B4=2B_4 = 2B4​=2 souls are reaped. After this, LLL changes to 111.
For house 111, A1=5>1A_1 = 5 \gt 1A1​=5>1, so no souls are reaped. After this, LLL changes to 555.
For house 222, A2=4≤5A_2 = 4 \leq 5A2​=4≤5, so B2=1B_2 = 1B2​=1 soul is reaped. After this, LLL changes to 444.
For house 555, A5=3≤4A_5 = 3 \leq 4A5​=3≤4, so B5=4B_5 = 4B5​=4 souls are reaped. After this, LLL changes to 333, and the process is done.

In total, 1+2+4=71+2+4 = 71+2+4=7 souls are reaped.
Test case 222: The Reaper can visit houses in any order, as long as 555 is visited before 222.
In this case, one optimal order is to visit 1→5→4→2→31\to 5\to 4 \to 2 \to 31→5→4→2→3, which reaps 10+2+4=1610+2+4 = 1610+2+4=16 souls.
It can be proved that this is the maximum possible.
Test case 333: It can be proved that we can not reap more than 313131 souls.
